- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/xPHuLKwAScSPw2fHBGAY_IMG_3211.png](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/xPHuLKwAScSPw2fHBGAY_IMG_3211.png)
- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/umwDKQvjQEG3mOUKGfOl_IMG_3212.png](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/umwDKQvjQEG3mOUKGfOl_IMG_3212.png)
- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/k3NfVTRtT3evojsJ53gK_IMG_3203.png](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/k3NfVTRtT3evojsJ53gK_IMG_3203.png)
- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/60xZHmzYSKSoBDVh0N02_IMG_3161.png](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/60xZHmzYSKSoBDVh0N02_IMG_3161.png)

---

##    10\. HOW TO CREATE MEDIA THAT'S A TROJAN HORSE FOR YOUR ARTISTIC VISION 

#  **PART I: MEDIA VS. CONTENT**  


People who use Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube aren't making art.  


They're creating content.  


But how about ARTISTS who use these same platforms to promote their art?   


Aren't THEY creating content also?  


It depends.  


According to Merriam-Webster dictionary:

Content = material, such as writing, video, or music that appears on a website or other electronic platform  


Media = forms of expression used to communicate ideas or feelings to a group of people   
  
Notice the subtle difference between these two words?

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/NNahfxORBG3szdTJPx7i_contentVSmediaTHREAD3.png)  


The definition of 'content' emphasizes "material that appears on a website". It lives and dies online.  


Whereas the definition of 'media' emphasizes "communication and expression". It's not limited to appearing online.  


The person "creating content" FOR Tiktok doesn't have the same intention as an artist "creating media" ON Tiktok.  


Changing the words you use to describe what you do, changes the nature of what you create in the process.  


People' creating content' are making sludge for the platforms. The platforms keep making money in exchange for providing a place for you to satisfy their "validation and attention" addiction.  


Artists' creating media' are using their chosen forms of expression to communicate to a group of people. They're releasing Trojan horses of ideas into the masses.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/uE66PTRo4xKG2Yk0QvX0_arnold_daniel.png)

#####  [@arnold_daniel](https://www.instagram.com/arnold_daniel/) If your visuals are worth 1000 words, then you don't need 1000 words in the caption. But everyone else... Give us context.

  


Content creators' interactions are always transactional.  


But artists interactions should always be about connection.   


How do you make sure this comes through?

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/96ktJNIbSRKVVf0VbjWi_adrodragynous.png)  


Start by refusing to call what you make "content".  


From now on call what you share on social media (video, photographs, and audio)… MEDIA.   


MEDIA = trojan horses for your artistic message.

  


#  **PART II: HOW TO CREATE MEDIA FOR YOUR ONLINE PRESENCE**  


MEDIA is not the art itself. It's a representation of the art that you share online to connect with your people.  


MEDIA = a picture you took of your painting. Or a video of you making music. Or a JPG of your poem written out.   


MEDIA is how you bridge the gap between what you do (your art) and the audience you cherish.  


When MEDIA is well done, this bridge is just as thoughtful and creative as the art itself. It may even become art in it's own right.  


When poorly done, it's… content.  


How do you make sure you're creating media? (assuming the art itself already has a vision behind it, of course)

  


##  **1\. TAKE TIME TO CREATE SOMETHING WORTH PEOPLE'S ATTENTION**  


Never post to post.  


Consider what kind of media (something that contains your message) would be worth people's attention.  


Then take the necessary time to make it.  


Often it's coming up with the initial idea that takes hours, but the execution only takes 5 minutes. That's ok.  


As long as you work on making it undeniable.  


Every post you share should be a microdose of your artistic vision. ([See World Building modules](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/enroll/lectures/23551748))

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/5Zr0WUXRF2jcIiZmkO8U_ethelcain.png)  


#####  [@mothercain](https://www.instagram.com/mothercain/?hl=en) photo dumps are a branding tour-de-force

  


Even if you're doing a "photo dump". Even if you're posting a "thirst trap". Be subversive. Make it a Trojan horse for something way deeper… 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/s33OS26Ryue85H5JzCcj_sighswoon.png)  


#####  [@sighswoon](https://www.instagram.com/sighswoon/) reels us in with "thirst traps" and memes but always delivers food for the soul

##   


## 2\. ALWAYS GIVE CONTEXT WHEN YOU SHARE ONLINE  


Sharing content without context shows your audience that you take their attention for granted.   


You're not Kylie Jenner.  


Celebrities are so famous that everything they do automatically has context. Their audience knows so much about them that anything they put online has an embedded narrative.  


You're not there yet.  


You're a new artist hoping to find your fans. You need to take steps so your future ride or dies "see" you.

Don't be lazy. Don't post something they can easily scroll past. Post thiings to make a connection.  


No one wants to see random iPhone b-roll video of your "art thing". That's content. If you want to make it MEDIA, tell us a story. Give us context.

Make it easy for people to resonate with what you're communicating.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/ZLtGQ1NSbibJZ0RN2RoH_bradleycopelandstudioCOMP.png)  


##### @[bradleycopelandstudio](https://www.instagram.com/bradleycopelandstudio/) There is no confusion when you land on Bradley Copeland's page what she does, and what she stands for. She uses her online presence to create a world that supports her values, belief and art.

  


![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/CzqbRPzRPuV217HxiX51_bradleycopelandWORDS.png)

##### @[bradleycopelandstudio](https://www.instagram.com/bradleycopelandstudio/) giving us context in a clear concise way.

  


Artists send me links all the time without explanation… Like I'm supposed to click on it, get blown away, and praise them for being a genius.  


This shows a lack of awareness.  


You art doesn't "automatically" matter to anyone. Not even your family. Not even your friends.  


You have to make it matter by showing you much you care about it FIRST.  


You do that by giving us context.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/pimMy5fbTFaR6Yrg3YJk_thesundropgarden.png)  


![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/3RaxbFgWRuqPbnLi4dHp_thesundropgardenWORDS.png)

##### [@thesundropgarden](https://www.instagram.com/thesundropgarden/)

  


No one can guess that the person in the picture is a stranger you just met. You have to tell us.  


No one can guess how you felt while you were there. You have to tell us.  


No one can guess that "this moment was when everything changed". You have to tell us.

Give us the back story to transport us into the world you're building.   


Do it with the caption, within the video, or however your creativity tells you to do it. But always...   


Tell us why it matters.  


Make it impossible for us not to see your genius.  


Make it impossible for us not to want to share it with others.  


Put the art in a frame.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Kj0AppZSQkm0YDWcuWlj_fairytailcrossing.png)

##### @[fairytailcrossing](https://www.instagram.com/fairytailcrossing/) Just because you have a 9 to 5 job doesn't mean you can't advance your art. Aren't you creative? Find a way.  


## 3\. STFU  


Stop with your un-edited word diarrhea.   


Run-on sentences isn't more "raw and in the moment". It's lazy rationalization for being sloppy.  


Be respectful of your audience's attention.  


Treat the first thing you write as the first draft it is. Edit it to make sure it communicates something powerful. Let your words sit for a few hours. Come back to it later so you can make sure it reads well.  


Save the stream of consciousness for your poetry. For captions and explanations— keep it direct and truthful. 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/2lKqcaPASGUO5qtGi0MZ_favelabaaby.png)  


##### [@favelababy](https://www.instagram.com/favelababy/)

  


How to best communicate:   


  1. \- Use short sentences.
  2. \- Keep it conversational (don't go into bullshit "writer mode").
  3. \- Highlight emotions. People don't care about what you did… They care about how you FELT doing what you did.
  4. \- Leave them wanting more.



![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OyIjerxSh2HYamSrfCAp_haniakanye.png)

#####  [@haniarani](https://www.instagram.com/haniarani/) [@kanyewest](https://www.instagram.com/kanyewest/)  


##   


## 4\. INVITE YOUR PEOPLE TO TAKE ACTION OFTEN.  


Being online is taking time away from you making art.  


Being online is interfering with your ability to enter flow.

So if you HAVE to be online, it had better be for a good reason.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OowttESQl2PpKAHHU45I_fariha_roisin.png)  


##### [@fariha_roisin](https://www.instagram.com/fariha_roisin/)

  


GOOD REASON = Helping you elevate your artistic career.  


GOOD REASON = Interacting with your audience.  


Like asking them who you should collaborate with next.   


Like polling them to find out where their head is at.  


Like sending them to sign up to your newsletter.

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/VeMVK50RjilUfHYmZTrT_truealevtina.png)  


##### @[truealevtina](https://www.instagram.com/truealevtina/) is a Ukrainian anti-war artist-activist. While you overthink about if you're "good enough"... Other artists out there are burning with so much fire, they expressing the raw emotion of what they believe in. 

  


People are scrolling past 500 videos and photos a day. Much of it is baseless, toxic, content.  


So when you create media for these platforms, make sure you create work that reflects that you understand this.  


Artists are competing against the lowest common denominator "content creators" who already know all this stuff.  


So you're gonna have to work twice as hard to create compelling media for the ages. 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/onXY54GBS6OzsqnF4vV0_earlyrupi.png)  


##### Early [@rupikaur](https://www.instagram.com/rupikaur_/) changing the game. Making social media work for HER.

  


## 5\. CREATE YOUR OWN VISUAL LANGUAGE

Did you notice something that ALL the above examples have?

All these artists have created their own visual language with their media.

You instantly recognize their posts among all the copycat stuff online. They have a very particular way of expressing themselves online that sets them apart.

So while you may be tempted to use those same Tiktok fonts, and caption styles as everyone else-- It will date everything you do.

Content.

Question every choice you make artistically even though this isn't even the art-- It's just to promote the art. But put the same care into it and your promotion itself will become artful.

Does this sound like too much work?  


Well, that "work" is the difference between hearing crickets when you post-- or curating a successful career for yourself.

I find it very taxing to think and curate visually.. I'm good with words and sonics being in music ofc but visually I feel years behind. It's not like I don't try, I do get by as a matter of fact. But I haven't able to find my 'voice' like that visually. Deep down I want to create something very distinct(I try to move away from the trending aesthetics, pick some off kilter things I like the most, etc.) but no concrete aesthetic of my own as of yet. Or maybe I'm not posting enough.. and that it's all in my head. Idk. Any advice would be very much welcome.

it's so funny that I think more/treat the process of posting on my personal account like art way more than i do on my art page because i fell into the idea that i had to express my art as content to get it seen . i never liked it when i saw pieces of media that made me feel something but then had nothing in the caption or something so detached from it-never liked doing that for myself but i'm changing that now, expressing myself creatively now, when i *am* online <3

When we got back to our beach house hose colors must have resonated with me. All of a sudden when smoking in the back yard I could see these colors everywhere and they were matching with my friend erins vibe. We started talking about the life of the creator and how being an artist full time allows me to see things differently.

Eventually I think I honed in on what the art required

  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/2ckd6eBTpl6nOYUydyen8ZaRTz0=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/wLcE9gRnGlkihwFJJZAn_Clip2Comic-2023-07-04-21-41-30.jpeg) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/wLcE9gRnGlkihwFJJZAn_Clip2Comic-2023-07-04-21-41-30.jpeg)

![Teach_or_Trav](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/5c29a530dbdde8f427092cc47f0219d4?d=mm&s=70)

Teach_or_Trav

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/46904991/comments/21180563)

We started shooting right there, getting high as hell passing the j back and forth. Erin became someone different this day. It was like meeting a stranger in my own backyard. IT turns out she really understood the vibe I am creating with the way I create. She started to get super focused on getting the best shots possible and then I went started shooting and editing in my style on the spot to capture the golden hour hues. Then I started iterating, adding music and posting on my tiktok story while the iron was still hot

* * *

  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/zEqTnSfSiPXqM4aqH8bjNcPsRyI=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/0mPx676VSryFiJgXvmuH_IMG_3200.png) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/0mPx676VSryFiJgXvmuH_IMG_3200.png)
  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/B5ZQrWPWanzmY2cM9tRgvpdQ_68=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/xPHuLKwAScSPw2fHBGAY_IMG_3211.png) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/xPHuLKwAScSPw2fHBGAY_IMG_3211.png)
  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/hCMmgzwQhr06ysw9F507847Ytic=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/umwDKQvjQEG3mOUKGfOl_IMG_3212.png) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/umwDKQvjQEG3mOUKGfOl_IMG_3212.png)

Teach_or_Trav

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/46904991/comments/21178024)

ok ok, so yea im still learning. I have found that when I tell my story however, even speaking up in chats and announcing what I do and who I am almost alwats yields positive results for my Art as well. AS I have been telling my story I went back to the source and started asking my childhood friends about all of our stories. Kelly and I were reminiscing in 7/11 when I saw this funny Mr. beast bar. I stopped to take a pic in my styl. I must have really been feeling the vibe of that wrapper because.

* * *

  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/tEKb8GqtwivMi44UunupAteZOjo=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/60xZHmzYSKSoBDVh0N02_IMG_3161.png) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/60xZHmzYSKSoBDVh0N02_IMG_3161.png)

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/46904991/comments/20584446)

You're right. You need to be communicating something. BUT... It doesn't have to be complicated at all. It just needs to be something you believe in with your heart and soul.
